title: Switching to overlay2 storage driver causes new problems with podman 3.2.2
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

overlay2 stopped working

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
After update to the latest 3.2.2 and buildah 1.21.1  certain features stopped working if overlay2 specified as storage driver.
Command _podman info_ no longer works unless prefixed with sudo.  I cannot build a container either. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Change storage.conf driver from overlay to overlay2. Execute podman system reset to cleanup and remove local .cache and .configs related to containers

2.  Run _podman info_ as non-root user

3. Also  try building image with 
podman build --format=docker  -t myrepo.io/xyz:latest . 

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman ps
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: kernel too old to provide multiple lowers feature for overlay: driver not supported
```
This only happens when run as non root but works fine when I add sudo

Also 
```
sudo podman build --log-level=debug --format=docker -t docker.xcastlabs.net/mysql/mysql8-server:1.1 -t docker.xcastlabs.net/mysql/mysql8-server:latest .
```
ERRO[0007] error deleting build container "e9440197c0f1d13958efbc54c14c4c801191e37fea47c6ce6111daf9a0547ec0": identifier is not a container 
DEBU[0007] Failed to cleanup intermediate containers: identifier is not a container 
Error: identifier is not a container: error preparing container for next step: error creating build container: error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "879b09f3be658711c7df107e0cd3c3dd75f7316257d3d9ae1d3aaf387ac5c301": Stat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/d3608bc9c34af538e0e57cba7c5dc217132635533ead3cfacbc0aa83f06592d8: no such file or directory

However: 
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/d3608bc9c34af538e0e57cba7c5dc217132635533ead3cfacbc0aa83f06592d8 
is located in
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay2/d3608bc9c34af538e0e57cba7c5dc217132635533ead3cfacbc0aa83f06592d8
as it should, but podman or buildah looking for it in /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/


**Describe the results you expected:**
A successful execution of podman info and successful build of the image.  As I said, going back to overlay makes things to work again.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.2
API Version:  3.2.2
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64

      host_id: 296608
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.4.0-45-generic
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body: @rhatdan PTAL
If you change it back to overlay does it work correctly?  overlay == overlay2, there is no difference in Podman.  We only use the overlay2 name for compatibility with Docker.
@rhatdan That is correct it is working with overlay.  However overlay2  was properly working  with previous versions.  Even 3.2.1, I believe.

I have no idea why this would not be working with overlay2.  I tried this on my Fedora machine and everything works fine.
Only thing I can think of is, some override check is failing when driver is overlay2

https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/966 might fix this, since we really do not want to deal with overlay2 at all.  Docker made a mistake when they renamed the driver.  No one uses the original overlay driver, so when we started making our container tools we just aliased the driver names together.

@rhatdan I tried it both on Ubuntu  20.04 and centos 7.9 with our nix static podman and buildah.  However, I will wait for #966  to clear the merge and test it then.  My problem with just going back to overlay  is I have to run podman system reset on multiple nodes.  It requires restarting bunch of containers and re-downloading bunch of images.
You could just set up a sympolic link between the two directories.

I could do that, but to do that I have to stop all the containers anyway.  Otherwise real directory /var/lib/containers/overlay is still in use and cannot be deleted before I could symlink it overlay2
@rhatdan I guess containers/storage#966 has not been merged so far.  Is it going to be or you guys had other thoughts about the issue?
We have to hold off until we are ready to ship podman 4. since this is a breaking change.  It will cause the same issue you are having.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is still an issue.

> I could do that, but to do that I have to stop all the containers anyway. Otherwise real directory /var/lib/containers/overlay is still in use and cannot be deleted before I could symlink it overlay2

you'd still need to do a `system reset` since the driver has changed, and Podman will complain with:

```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "overlay2" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve
```

so I am not sure the fix for containers/storage will be helpful for your use case.
Also happening for me on 3.0.1, on Debian 11
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Fwiw, I found that this caused a DoS for other accounts on the system. None of them could start or create containers and I ended up restoring the system from backup to fix it. I wasn't 110% sure I hadn't done something else to cause the problem, but I think it's worth investigating
Were you using overlay2?

```
$ podman system reset
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
Error: vfs driver does not support overlay.mount_program options
```

Sorry if this is a separate issue. I think I was linked here from IRC when I described it. I'll try to reproduce it in a VM.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe this is now fixed in the main branch.  You should get a loud warning if you are attempting to use overlay2.
